rationale.R1.2: The broad request to check the passage centrally includes verifying whether the argument chain from model agreement to predictive skill and planning confidence is internally consistent.
rationale.R1.3: The passage makes quantitative claims about all six regions and headline summary statistics, so checking whether those claims align with the reported regional results is central.
rationale.RX.1: A check should flag where uncertainty is material, especially because two regions show substantial disagreement and are still part of the evaluated panel.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is load-bearing because the artifact draws strong conclusions about prediction, physical constraint, and adaptation use from inter-model agreement.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must remain limited to identifying the responsibilities implied by the delegation rather than performing the requested check.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is active because the artifact concerns climate adaptation planning, where overstated confidence could affect real-world decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are active because any responsibility to check the passage depends on tracking claims back to the numbers and definitions provided in the artifact.
